generated_oml:
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor modeled as IFC type IFCTEMPERATURESENSOR in the BIM model, enriched with Instance General UUID metadata."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureTelemetry
]

instance PresenceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Presence sensor modeled as IFC type IFCPRESENCESENSOR in the BIM model, enriched with Instance General UUID metadata."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PresenceTelemetry
]

instance LightFixtureActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting fixture actuator modeled as IFC type IFCLIGHTFIXTURETYPE, exposing on/off and intensity/color‑temperature control over MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightActuationStatus
]

instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC actuator modeled as IFC type IFCHVAC, exposing setpoint and fan speed controls over MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACActuationStatus
]

instance TemperatureTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureData
    base:desc "Time‑stamped temperature data transmitted via Eclipse Hono MQTT, at least once delivery, 1 s heartbeat."
]

instance PresenceTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PresenceSensor
    DTDFVocab:fromData PresenceData
    base:desc "Occupancy status transmitted via Eclipse Hono MQTT, at least once delivery, 1 s heartbeat."
]

instance LightActuationStatus : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightFixtureActuator
    DTDFVocab:fromData LightActuationData
    base:desc "Current state of the light fixture (on/off, intensity, color–temperature) reported via Ditto MQTT."
]

instance HVACActuationStatus : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACActuator
    DTDFVocab:fromData HVACActuationData
    base:desc "Current state of the HVAC actuator (setpoint, fan speed) reported via Ditto MQTT."
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated temperature measurement with unit checks and bounds enforcement via Ditto policy."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPlatform
]

instance PresenceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated presence detection with unit checks and bounds enforcement via Ditto policy."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPlatform
]

instance LightActuationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated light fixture status with policy enforcement."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPlatform
]

instance HVACActuationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated HVAC status with policy enforcement."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPlatform
]

instance MDEFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model‑Driven Engineering framework that extracts structured artefacts from enriched BIM models and produces deployable twin components."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, OptimizationService, PredictionService, VisualizationService, DeploymentService
]

instance HonoConnector : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono MQTT bridge that ingests telemetry from physical devices and forwards it to Eclipse Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlService
]

instance DittoPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto platform handling device registry, state persistence, and policy enforcement."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlService, VisualizationService
]

instance UnrealEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine 5 rendering the 3‑D virtual twin using photometric metadata translated from the BIM."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance CustomTooling : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom tooling for device modelling, control logic configuration, and plugin development within the deployment pipeline."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeploymentService
]

instance DeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Deployment pipeline that consumes JSON twin definitions, creates Ditto twins, configures Eclipse Hono MQTT bridges, and starts cloud‑native containers."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous synchronization of live building‑sensor data via Eclipse Hono and Ditto, providing up‑to‑date status of environmental conditions, occupancy, and equipment performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentalStatusInsight, OccupancyInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated recommendation engine driven by predictive analytics that identifies operational adjustments (e.g., HVAC set‑points) to improve energy efficiency and occupant comfort."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight, HVACRecommendationInsight
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Forecasting of building performance indicators using data‑driven models embedded in the twin, enabling early detection of anomalies or maintenance needs."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight, MaintenancePredictionInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive control interface that permits facility managers and building‑control engineers to issue commands to actuators and devices."
    DTDFVocab:provides DirectCommandAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3‑D representation delivered via Unreal Engine 5, enabling stakeholders to explore the digital twin in real‑time with realistic rendering of building geometry and state information."
    DTDFVocab:provides UE5Visualization
]

instance EnvironmentalStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current environmental conditions (temperature, humidity) extracted from real‑time sensor telemetry."
]

instance OccupancyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time occupancy state determined from presence sensor telemetry."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of current energy consumption patterns relative to optimal profiles."
]

instance HVACRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Suggested HVAC set‑point adjustments derived from optimization analysis."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of anomalies in building performance indicators such as temperature drift or equipment degradation."
]

instance MaintenancePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance schedule based on forecasted performance trends."
]

instance UE5Visualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time 3‑D visualization of the building state within Unreal Engine 5."
]

instance DeploymentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Status and logs of twin deployment, including patch updates and OSGi bundle registrations."
]

instance DirectCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to actuate devices (e.g., toggle light, set HVAC set‑point)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands originate from the digital twin interface and are transmitted back to physical devices through the same Hono/Ditto channel. The runtime engine accepts command messages, serialises them into the format required by the underlying BCS middleware (e.g., Hono command messages), and forwards them to the device via Ditto’s command endpoint. Upon receipt, devices acknowledge execution back to Hono, which routes the acknowledgement to the twin, allowing the digital model to be updated with the confirmed state. The system reconciles conflicting commands by ensuring command sequencing via Ditto’s versioned payloads and only accepts the latest command per device."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Real‑time synchronization is achieved via a 1‑second heartbeat mechanism that drives continuous data exchange between physical building sensors and the digital twin using Eclipse Hono and Eclipse Ditto. The initial deployment pipeline requires 14.53 seconds, composed of 5.53 seconds for model checking, 8.7 seconds for model parsing, and 0.3 seconds for initialization and setup."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation follows a modular, service‑oriented microservices pattern. Enriched BIM models (created in Autodesk Revit) are processed by an MDE engine that generates JSON‑based runtime artifacts. These artifacts are automatically deployed via the framework’s low‑overhead CI/CD pipeline (C2), provisioning an Eclipse Ditto instance for twin storage and an Eclipse Hono broker for real‑time data ingestion. The central DT Framework orchestrates communication among all twin instances and external services, exposing REST/HTTP, WebSocket, and MQTT endpoints. Runtime configuration management allows services and plugins to be added or updated on the fly without full redeployment, preserving system uptime. An immersive visualization layer rendered in Unreal Engine 5 provides Facility Managers with a real‑time 3‑D view of monitored states and control capabilities."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The framework defines a five‑phase lifecycle for a building Digital Twin:
(1) Design‑time modelling – Building Control Engineers embed twinable devices directly in Autodesk Revit as custom ‘family’ templates, extend each element with semantic metadata (IfcGeneralisationType, property sets and a mandatory Instance General UUID) and set the IFC type (e.g., IFCLIGHTFIXTURETYPE).  
(2) Semantic extraction – the enriched IFC file is validated by an IFC‑based model checker that ensures all required metadata are present.  
(3) Transformation – a model‑to‑model engine maps the semantic entities to JSON definitions that conform to the Eclipse Ditto SD‑API and generates DSL artefacts for runtime use.  
(4) One‑click deployment – a containerised orchestrator consumes the artefacts, instantiates the corresponding Ditto digital twins, configures Eclipse Hono MQTT bridges, and launches all runtime services through a single API call.  
(5) Runtime operations – the twin receives continuous bidirectional telemetry via Hono / MQTT, visualises the state in a UE5 3‑D scene, and is governed by a runtime configuration manager that permits on‑the‑fly parameter updates and plugin injections.
Transition management is handled by meta‑models preserving links between BIM elements, semantic artefacts and runtime instances; version tags on the IFC model and artefact catalogue enable traceability. Governance is expressed through role‑based access control (Control‑Engineer, Facility‑Manager, DT‑Engineer) and policy enforcement for lifecycle change requests."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is quantified using the Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric, computed as the product of timeliness, reliability, and availability. In a 12‑minute test with a 1 s heartbeat, timeliness achieved 0.997, reliability 1.0, and availability 1.0, resulting in an ODTE score of 0.997, indicating near‑perfect entanglement. Validation of the BIM model is performed by a dedicated IFC model checker that enforces the presence of required metadata such as Instance General UUID. Runtime data integrity is verified through Eclipse Ditto’s policy engine, which enforces value ranges and units on incoming telemetry and outgoing actuator commands. Fault tolerance is achieved via Hono’s at‑least‑once message delivery and Ditto’s persistent event store, enabling recovery from transient network failures. The modular architecture supports runtime configuration management and service isolation, allowing continuous validation and refinement of both models and data."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The twin infrastructure is built on an Eclipse Hono data‑hub for device ingress/egress and Eclipse Ditto for twin state management and event publication. Hono aggregates device telemetry and forwards it to Ditto, which exposes a JSON‑based API for twin updates and command routing. The architecture is a publish/subscribe mesh: device data is pushed to Hono, Hono pushes updates to Ditto, and twin instances subscribe to Ditto topics for real‑time state changes. The system thus provides a scalable, event‑driven connectivity layer with high availability through Hono’s clustered deployment and Ditto’s replication."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The documents do not specify whether the Hono/Ditto brokers, UE5 runtime, or supporting services are hosted on cloud, edge, or on‑premise infrastructure, nor do they provide details on computational or storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, deployment models, or availability guarantees."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The framework establishes horizontal integration through standardized interfaces: data ingestion and event publication via Eclipse Hono (supporting MQTT and CoAP) and twin management via Eclipse Ditto (exposing WebSocket, HTTP, and CoAP APIs). External systems—including BIM authoring tools such as Autodesk Revit, simulation engines, and building automation platforms—interact with the twin through these protocols. The twin store represents asset data in JSON‑LD format aligned with IFC semantic extensions, ensuring interoperability with other BIM‑centric Digital Twin ecosystems. The architecture supports horizontal scaling by deploying multiple Ditto replicas or Hono gateways, and it provides open extension points that enable integration with external enterprise systems and separate Digital Twin ecosystems."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The solution complies with IFC 4x3 (ISO 16739) for BIM exchange and enriches models with IFC‑based metadata such as Instance General UUID and IFC‑type identifiers (e.g., IFCLIGHTFIXTURETYPE).  It inter‑operates with proprietary BIM tools (Autodesk Revit, Navisworks) through IFC export.  Real‑time communication follows MQTT/AMQP specifications, implemented via Eclipse Hono whose message schema follows the Open Telemetry semantic convention.  Digital twin data is published through Eclipse Ditto following the Ditto API specification (OpenAPI 3).  The framework adopts ISO 21001 for building asset management and integrates with the IoTivity and OCF interoperability frameworks.  Service integration follows the OSGi modularity specification, and all artefacts are versioned following Semantic Versioning 2.0 guidelines."
]
